title: NATO SNMG2 patrols Mediterranean monitoring Russian shadow fleet STS operations
summary: NATO Standing Maritime Group 2 conducts maritime security patrols in the Mediterranean Sea, monitoring Russian shadow fleet ship-to-ship transfer operations off Greece. Surveillance focuses on sanctions evasion activity.
contents:
# NATO SNMG2 Monitors Russian Shadow Fleet in Mediterranean

## Mission Overview
NATO's Standing Naval Maritime Group 2 (SNMG2) conducts ongoing maritime security patrols in the Mediterranean Sea, with particular focus on monitoring Russian shadow fleet ship-to-ship transfer operations.

## Key Activity
Recent satellite and surface intelligence detected STS transfers involving Russian shadow fleet vessels off the coast of Greece. An April 2026 fire on the vessel Canis Power during STS operations off Crete prompted Greek Coast Guard evacuation of 22 crew members and oil spill containment.

## Patrol Focus Areas
- Central and Eastern Mediterranean maritime lanes
- STS transfer hotspots off Greece and the Laconian Gulf
- Counter-terrorism surveillance under Operation Sea Guardian
- Monitoring of suspicious vessel behavior and dark fleet activity

## Force Composition
SNMG2 integrates frigates and destroyers from multiple NATO allied navies, providing continuous maritime domain awareness across the alliance's southern flank.

## Tags
nato, snmg2, mediterranean, maritime-security, russia, shadow-fleet, sanctions, sea-guardian
